query: What Dominion Energy commercial customer service class pays the highest demand charge in Virginia?
final_answer:
Based on a thorough review of Dominion Energy Virginia's (Virginia Electric and Power Company) current commercial rate schedules effective January 1, 2026, here is a comprehensive comparison:

---

## Dominion Energy Virginia – Commercial Demand Charges by Service Class

| Rate Schedule | Customer Type | Demand Charge |
|---|---|---|
| **GS-1** | Small General Service (<30 kW) | No demand charge |
| **GS-2** | Intermediate General Service (30–500 kW) | $4.963/kW (Distribution) |
| **Schedule 6TS** | Thermal Storage (≥50 kW) | Up to $4.112/kW (Distribution) + $7.335/kW (Generation) |
| **GS-3** | Large General Service, Secondary Voltage (≥500 kW) | $3.316/kW (Distribution) + **$8.976/kW On-Peak Generation** |
| **GS-4** | Large General Service, Primary Voltage (≥500 kW) | **$9.280/kW** (Transmission Service Voltage Generation Demand) |
| **Schedule 10** | Large General Service, TOU (≥500 kW) | $2.717–$3.316/kW (Distribution) |

---

## 🏆 Answer: **Schedule GS-4 — Large General Service, Primary Voltage**

**Schedule GS-4** carries the **highest demand charge** among Dominion Energy Virginia's commercial customer service classes, at **$9.280 per kW** for customers served at transmission service voltage. This schedule applies to non-residential customers served at **transmission or primary voltage** (≥69 kV) with a peak demand of at least **500 kW** in at least three billing months.

While GS-3 (secondary voltage) carries an on-peak generation demand charge of $8.976/kW, GS-4's transmission-level demand charge of **$9.280/kW** is the single highest demand rate across all commercial service classes. This reflects the cost of serving large industrial/commercial customers at higher voltage levels, though customers at primary/transmission voltage also benefit from lower distribution-level losses and often lower per-kWh energy charges.

tool_input.query: Dominion Energy Virginia commercial customer demand charge service class
Dominion Energy Virginia commercial customer service class highest demand charge rate schedule

steps.content: Now I have a good picture of the commercial rate schedules. Let me pull the GS-4 (Large General Service Primary Voltage) PDF to compare its demand charges, as it serves larger customers at primary voltage and likely has different rates than GS-2 and GS-3.

|---|---|---|

| **Schedule 6TS** | Thermal Storage (≥50 kW) | Up to $4.112/kW (Distribution) + $7.335/kW (Generation) |
| **GS-3** | Large General Service, Secondary Voltage (≥500 kW) | $3.316/kW (Distribution) + **$8.976/kW On-Peak Generation** |
| **GS-4** | Large General Service, Primary Voltage (≥500 kW) | **$9.280/kW** (Transmission Service Voltage Generation Demand) |
| **Schedule 10** | Large General Service, TOU (≥500 kW) | $2.717–$3.316/kW (Distribution) |

---

## 🏆 Answer: **Schedule GS-4 — Large General Service, Primary Voltage**

**Schedule GS-4** carries the **highest demand charge** among Dominion Energy Virginia's commercial customer service classes, at **$9.280 per kW** for customers served at transmission service voltage. This schedule applies to non-residential customers served at **transmission or primary voltage** (≥69 kV) with a peak demand of at least **500 kW** in at least three billing months.

While GS-3 (secondary voltage) carries an on-peak generation demand charge of $8.976/kW, GS-4's transmission-level demand charge of **$9.280/kW** is the single highest demand rate across all commercial service classes. This reflects the cost of serving large industrial/commercial customers at higher voltage levels, though customers at primary/transmission voltage also benefit from lower distribution-level losses and often lower per-kWh energy charges.
